name: Extrusion
summary: Represents an extrusion, or objects such as beams or linearly extruded elements, that can be represented by profile curves and two miter planes at the extremes.

constructors.name: Extrusion()
constructors.summary: Initializes a new instance of the Extrusion class.

params.name: info
params.doc: The serialization data.

params.name: context
params.doc: The serialization context stream.

methods.name: AddInnerProfile
methods.summary: Adds an inner profile.

params.name: innerProfile
params.doc: Closed curve in the XY plane or a 2d curve.

returns.doc: true if the profile was set.

methods.name: ClosestPoint
methods.signature: public bool ClosestPoint(Point3d testPoint, out double u, out double v)
methods.summary: Input the parameters of the point on the surface that is closest to testPoint.

params.name: testPoint
params.doc: A point to test against.

params.name: u
params.doc: U parameter of the surface that is closest to testPoint.

params.name: v
params.doc: V parameter of the surface that is closest to testPoint.

returns.doc: true on success, false on failure.

methods.name: ClosestSide
methods.signature: public IsoStatus ClosestSide(double u, double v)
methods.summary: Gets the side that is closest, in terms of 3D-distance, to a U and V parameter.

params.name: u
params.doc: A u parameter.

params.name: v
params.doc: A v parameter.

returns.doc: A side.

methods.name: ComponentIndex
methods.summary: If this piece of geometry is a component in something larger, like a BrepEdge in a Brep, then this function returns the component index.

returns.doc: This object's component index. If this object is not a sub-piece of a larger geometric entity, then the returned index has m_type = ComponentIndex.InvalidType and m_index = -1.

methods.name: ConstructConstObject
methods.signature: protected void ConstructConstObject(Object parentObject, int subobjectIndex)
methods.summary: Assigns a parent object and a sub-object index to this.

params.name: parentObject
params.doc: The parent object.

params.name: subobjectIndex
params.doc: The sub-object index.

methods.name: Create
methods.signature: public static Extrusion Create(Curve planarCurve, double height, bool cap)
methods.summary: Creates an extrusion of a 3d curve (which must be planar) and a height.

params.name: planarCurve
params.doc: Planar curve used as profile

params.name: height
params.doc: If the height > 0, the bottom of the extrusion will be in plane and the top will be height units above the plane. If the height < 0, the top of the extrusion will be in plane and the bottom will be height units below the plane. The plane used is the one that is returned from the curve's TryGetPlane function.

params.name: cap
params.doc: If the curve is closed and cap is true, then the resulting extrusion is capped.

returns.doc: If the input is valid, then a new extrusion is returned. Otherwise null is returned

methods.name: CreateBoxExtrusion
methods.signature: public static Extrusion CreateBoxExtrusion(Box box, bool cap = true)
methods.summary: Gets an extrusion from a box.

params.name: box
params.doc: IsValid must be true.

params.name: cap
params.doc: If true, the base and the top of the box will be capped. Defaults to true.

returns.doc: Extrusion on success. null on failure.

methods.name: CreateCylinderExtrusion
methods.signature: public static Extrusion CreateCylinderExtrusion(Cylinder cylinder, bool capBottom, bool capTop)
methods.summary: Gets an extrusion form of a cylinder.

params.name: cylinder
params.doc: IsFinite must be true.

params.name: capBottom
params.doc: If true, the end at cylinder.Height1 will be capped.

params.name: capTop
params.doc: If true, the end at cylinder.Height2 will be capped.

returns.doc: Extrusion on success. null on failure.

methods.name: CreatePipeExtrusion
methods.signature: public static Extrusion CreatePipeExtrusion(Cylinder cylinder, double otherRadius, bool capTop, bool capBottom)
methods.summary: Gets an extrusion form of a pipe.

params.name: cylinder
params.doc: IsFinite must be true.

params.name: otherRadius
params.doc: If cylinder.Radius is less than other radius, then the cylinder will be the inside of the pipe.

params.name: capTop
params.doc: If true, the end at cylinder.Height2 will be capped.

params.name: capBottom
params.doc: If true, the end at cylinder.Height1 will be capped.

returns.doc: Extrusion on success. null on failure.

methods.name: CurvatureAt
methods.signature: public SurfaceCurvature CurvatureAt(double u, double v)
methods.summary: Computes the curvature at the given UV coordinate.

params.name: u
params.doc: U parameter for evaluation.

params.name: v
params.doc: V parameter for evaluation.

returns.doc: Surface Curvature data for the point at UV or null on failure.

methods.name: Degree
methods.summary: Returns the maximum algebraic degree of any span (or a good estimate if curve spans are not algebraic).

params.name: direction
params.doc: 0 gets first parameter's domain, 1 gets second parameter's domain.

returns.doc: The maximum degree.

methods.name: Dispose()
methods.summary: Actively reclaims unmanaged resources that this instance uses.

methods.name: Dispose(Boolean)
methods.summary: Overridden in order to destroy local display cache information

params.name: disposing
params.doc: True to release both managed and unmanaged resources; false to release only unmanaged resources

methods.name: Domain
methods.summary: Gets the domain in a direction.

params.name: direction
params.doc: 0 gets first parameter, 1 gets second parameter.

returns.doc: An interval value.

methods.name: Duplicate
methods.summary: Constructs a deep (full) copy of this object.

returns.doc: An object of the same type as this, with the same properties and behavior.

methods.name: DuplicateShallow
methods.summary: Constructs a light copy of this object. By "light", it is meant that the same underlying data is used until something is done to attempt to change it. For example, you could have a shallow copy of a very heavy mesh object and the same underlying data will be used when doing things like inspecting the number of faces on the mesh. If you modify the location of one of the mesh vertices, the shallow copy will create a full duplicate of the underlying mesh data and the shallow copy will become a deep copy.

returns.doc: An object of the same type as this object. This behavior is overridden by implementing classes.

methods.name: EnsurePrivateCopy
methods.summary: If you want to keep a copy of this class around by holding onto it in a variable after a command completes, call EnsurePrivateCopy to make sure that this class is not tied to the document. You can call this function as many times as you want.

methods.name: Evaluate
methods.signature: public bool Evaluate(double u, double v, int numberDerivatives, out Point3d point, out Vector3d[] derivatives)
methods.summary: Evaluates a surface mathematically.
methods.remarks: The partial derivatives will be in the order {Su, Sv} for numberDerivatives = 1, {Su, Sv, Suu, Suv, Svv} for 2, {Su, Sv, Suu, Suv, Svv, Suuu, Suuv, Suvv, Svvv} for 3, and similar for higher counts.

params.name: u
params.doc: A U parameter.

params.name: v
params.doc: A V parameter.

params.name: numberDerivatives
params.doc: The number of derivatives.

params.name: point
params.doc: A point. This out parameter will be assigned during this call.

params.name: derivatives
params.doc: A vector array. This out parameter will be assigned during this call. This can be null.

returns.doc: true if the operation succeeded; false otherwise.

methods.name: Extend(Int32, Interval)
methods.signature: public bool Extend(int direction, Interval interval)
methods.summary: Analytically extends the surface to include the interval.

params.name: direction
params.doc: If 0, Surface.Domain(0) will include the interval. (the first surface parameter). If 1, Surface.Domain(1) will include the interval. (the second surface parameter).

params.name: interval
params.doc: If the interval is not included in surface domain, the surface will be extended so that its domain includes the interval. Note, this method will fail if the surface is closed in the specified direction.

returns.doc: True if successful, false otherwise.

methods.name: Extend(IsoStatus, Double, Boolean)
methods.signature: public Surface Extend(IsoStatus edge, double extensionLength, bool smooth)
methods.summary: Extends an untrimmed surface along one edge.

params.name: edge
params.doc: Edge to extend. Must be North, South, East, or West.

params.name: extensionLength
params.doc: distance to extend.

params.name: smooth
params.doc: true for smooth (C-infinity) extension. false for a C1- ruled extension.

returns.doc: New extended surface on success.

methods.name: Finalize
methods.summary: Passively reclaims unmanaged resources when the class user did not explicitly call Dispose().

methods.name: Fit
methods.signature: public Surface Fit(int uDegree, int vDegree, double fitTolerance)
methods.summary: Fits a new surface through an existing surface.

params.name: uDegree
params.doc: the output surface U degree. Must be bigger than 1.

params.name: vDegree
params.doc: the output surface V degree. Must be bigger than 1.

params.name: fitTolerance
params.doc: The fitting tolerance.

returns.doc: A surface, or null on error.

methods.name: FrameAt
methods.signature: public bool FrameAt(double u, double v, out Plane frame)
methods.summary: Computes the orient plane on a surface given a U and V parameter. This is the simple evaluation call with no error handling.

params.name: u
params.doc: A first parameter.

params.name: v
params.doc: A second parameter.

params.name: frame
params.doc: A frame plane that will be computed during this call.

returns.doc: true if this operation succeeded; otherwise false.

methods.name: GetBoundingBox(Boolean)
methods.summary: Bounding box solver. Gets the world axis aligned bounding box for the geometry.

params.name: accurate
params.doc: If true, a physically accurate bounding box will be computed. If not, a bounding box estimate will be computed. For some geometry types there is no difference between the estimate and the accurate bounding box. Estimated bounding boxes can be computed much (much) faster than accurate (or "tight") bounding boxes. Estimated bounding boxes are always similar to or larger than accurate bounding boxes.

returns.doc: The bounding box of the geometry in world coordinates or BoundingBox.Empty if not bounding box could be found.

methods.name: GetBoundingBox(Plane)
methods.summary: Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the plane aligned bounding box.

params.name: plane
params.doc: Orientation plane for BoundingBox.

returns.doc: A BoundingBox in plane coordinates.

methods.name: GetBoundingBox(Transform)
methods.summary: Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the world axis aligned bounding box for the transformed geometry.

params.name: xform
params.doc: Transformation to apply to object prior to the BoundingBox computation. The geometry itself is not modified.

returns.doc: The accurate bounding box of the transformed geometry in world coordinates or BoundingBox.Empty if not bounding box could be found.

methods.name: GetBoundingBox(Plane, Box)
methods.signature: public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(Plane plane, out Box worldBox)
methods.summary: Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the plane aligned bounding box.

params.name: plane
params.doc: Orientation plane for BoundingBox.

params.name: worldBox
params.doc: Aligned box in World coordinates.

returns.doc: A BoundingBox in plane coordinates.

methods.name: GetMesh
methods.summary: Obtains a reference to a specified type of mesh for this extrusion.

params.name: meshType
params.doc: The mesh type.

returns.doc: A mesh.

methods.name: GetNextDiscontinuity(Int32, Continuity, Double, Double, Double)
methods.signature: public bool GetNextDiscontinuity(int direction, Continuity continuityType, double t0, double t1, out double t)
methods.summary: Searches for a derivative, tangent, or curvature discontinuity.

params.name: direction
params.doc: If 0, then "u" parameter is checked. If 1, then the "v" parameter is checked.

params.name: continuityType
params.doc: The desired continuity.

params.name: t0
params.doc: Search begins at t0. If there is a discontinuity at t0, it will be ignored. This makes it possible to repeatedly call GetNextDiscontinuity and step through the discontinuities.

params.name: t1
params.doc: (t0 != t1) If there is a discontinuity at t1 is will be ignored unless c is a locus discontinuity type and t1 is at the start or end of the curve.

params.name: t
params.doc: if a discontinuity is found, then t reports the parameter at the discontinuity.

returns.doc: Parametric continuity tests c = (C0_continuous, ..., G2_continuous): TRUE if a parametric discontinuity was found strictly between t0 and t1. Note well that all curves are parametrically continuous at the ends of their domains. Locus continuity tests c = (C0_locus_continuous, ...,G2_locus_continuous): TRUE if a locus discontinuity was found strictly between t0 and t1 or at t1 is the at the end of a curve. Note well that all open curves (IsClosed()=false) are locus discontinuous at the ends of their domains. All closed curves (IsClosed()=true) are at least C0_locus_continuous at the ends of their domains.

methods.name: GetNextDiscontinuity(Int32, Continuity, Double, Double, Double, Double, Double)
methods.signature: public bool GetNextDiscontinuity(int direction, Continuity continuityType, double t0, double t1, double cosAngleTolerance, double curvatureTolerance, out double t)
methods.summary: Searches for a derivative, tangent, or curvature discontinuity.

params.name: direction
params.doc: If 0, then "u" parameter is checked. If 1, then the "v" parameter is checked.

params.name: continuityType
params.doc: The desired continuity.

params.name: t0
params.doc: Search begins at t0. If there is a discontinuity at t0, it will be ignored. This makes it possible to repeatedly call GetNextDiscontinuity and step through the discontinuities.

params.name: t1
params.doc: (t0 != t1) If there is a discontinuity at t1 is will be ignored unless c is a locus discontinuity type and t1 is at the start or end of the curve.

params.name: cosAngleTolerance
params.doc: default = cos(1 degree) Used only when continuityType is G1_continuous or G2_continuous. If the cosine of the angle between two tangent vectors is <= cos_angle_tolerance, then a G1 discontinuity is reported.

params.name: curvatureTolerance
params.doc: (default = ON_SQRT_EPSILON) Used only when continuityType is G2_continuous. If K0 and K1 are curvatures evaluated from above and below and |K0 - K1| > curvature_tolerance, then a curvature discontinuity is reported.

params.name: t
params.doc: if a discontinuity is found, then t reports the parameter at the discontinuity.

returns.doc: Parametric continuity tests c = (C0_continuous, ..., G2_continuous): TRUE if a parametric discontinuity was found strictly between t0 and t1. Note well that all curves are parametrically continuous at the ends of their domains. Locus continuity tests c = (C0_locus_continuous, ...,G2_locus_continuous): TRUE if a locus discontinuity was found strictly between t0 and t1 or at t1 is the at the end of a curve. Note well that all open curves (IsClosed()=false) are locus discontinuous at the ends of their domains. All closed curves (IsClosed()=true) are at least C0_locus_continuous at the ends of their domains.

methods.name: GetNurbsFormParameterFromSurfaceParameter
methods.signature: public bool GetNurbsFormParameterFromSurfaceParameter(double surfaceS, double surfaceT, out double nurbsS, out double nurbsT)
methods.summary: Translates a parameter from the current surface to the parameter space of the surface returned by ToNurbsSurface().

params.name: surfaceS
params.doc: The parameter in the S, or sometimes U, direction, of this surface.

params.name: surfaceT
params.doc: The parameter in the T, or sometimes V, direction of this surface.

params.name: nurbsS
params.doc: S on the NURBS form.

params.name: nurbsT
params.doc: T on the NURBS form.

returns.doc: True if the operation succeeded; otherwise, false.

methods.name: GetObjectData
methods.signature: public virtual void GetObjectData(SerializationInfo info, StreamingContext context)
methods.summary: Populates a System.Runtime.Serialization.SerializationInfo with the data needed to serialize the target object.

params.name: info
params.doc: The System.Runtime.Serialization.SerializationInfo to populate with data.

params.name: context
params.doc: The destination (see System.Runtime.Serialization.StreamingContext) for this serialization.

methods.name: GetPathPlane
methods.summary: Gets the 3D plane perpendicular to the path at a normalized path parameter.
methods.remarks: When no mitering is happening, GetPathPlane() and GetProfilePlane() return the same plane.

params.name: s
params.doc: 0.0 = starting profile 1.0 = ending profile.

returns.doc: A plane. The plane is Invalid on failure.

methods.name: GetProfilePlane
methods.summary: Gets the 3D plane containing the profile curve at a normalized path parameter.
methods.remarks: When no mitering is happening, GetPathPlane() and GetProfilePlane() return the same plane.

params.name: s
params.doc: 0.0 = starting profile 1.0 = ending profile.

returns.doc: A plane. The plane is Invalid on failure.

methods.name: GetProfileTransformation
methods.summary: Gets the transformation that maps the XY profile curve to its 3d location.

params.name: s
params.doc: 0.0 = starting profile 1.0 = ending profile.

returns.doc: A Transformation. The transform is Invalid on failure.

methods.name: GetSpanVector
methods.summary: Gets array of span "knots".

params.name: direction
params.doc: 0 gets first parameter's domain, 1 gets second parameter's domain.

returns.doc: An array with span vectors; or null on error.

methods.name: GetSurfaceParameterFromNurbsFormParameter
methods.signature: public bool GetSurfaceParameterFromNurbsFormParameter(double nurbsS, double nurbsT, out double surfaceS, out double surfaceT)
methods.summary: Translates a parameter from a value on the surface returned by ToNurbsSurface() to the current surface.

params.name: nurbsS
params.doc: The parameter in the S, or sometimes U, direction of the NURBS form surface.

params.name: nurbsT
params.doc: The parameter in the T, or sometimes V, direction of the NURBS form surface.

params.name: surfaceS
params.doc: S on this surface.

params.name: surfaceT
params.doc: T o n this surface.

returns.doc: True if the operation succeeded; otherwise, false.

methods.name: GetSurfaceSize
methods.signature: public bool GetSurfaceSize(out double width, out double height)
methods.summary: Gets an estimate of the size of the rectangle that would be created if the 3d surface where flattened into a rectangle.

params.name: width
params.doc: corresponds to the first surface parameter.

params.name: height
params.doc: corresponds to the second surface parameter.

returns.doc: true if successful.

methods.name: GetUserString
methods.summary: Gets user string from this geometry.

params.name: key
params.doc: id used to retrieve the string.

returns.doc: string associated with the key if successful. null if no key was found.

methods.name: GetUserStrings
methods.summary: Gets a copy of all (user key string, user value string) pairs attached to this geometry.

returns.doc: A new collection.

methods.name: GetWireframe
methods.summary: Constructs all the Wireframe curves for this Extrusion.

returns.doc: An array of Wireframe curves.

methods.name: HasNurbsForm
methods.summary: Is there a NURBS surface representation of this surface.

returns.doc: 0 unable to create NURBS representation with desired accuracy. 1 success - NURBS parameterization matches the surface's 2 success - NURBS point locus matches the surface's and the domain of the NURBS surface is correct. However, This surface's parameterization and the NURBS surface parameterization may not match. This situation happens when getting NURBS representations of surfaces that have a transcendental parameterization like spheres, cylinders, and cones.

methods.name: InterpolatedCurveOnSurface
methods.summary: Constructs an interpolated curve on a surface, using 3D points.

params.name: points
params.doc: A list, an array or any enumerable set of points.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: A tolerance value.

returns.doc: A new NURBS curve, or null on error.

methods.name: InterpolatedCurveOnSurfaceUV(IEnumerable<Point2d>, Double)
methods.summary: Returns a curve that interpolates points on a surface. The interpolant lies on the surface.

params.name: points
params.doc: List of at least two UV parameter locations on the surface.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: Tolerance used for the fit of the push-up curve. Generally, the resulting interpolating curve will be within tolerance of the surface.

returns.doc: A new NURBS curve if successful, or null on error.

methods.name: InterpolatedCurveOnSurfaceUV(IEnumerable<Point2d>, Double, Boolean, Int32)
methods.signature: public NurbsCurve InterpolatedCurveOnSurfaceUV(IEnumerable<Point2d> points, double tolerance, bool closed, int closedSurfaceHandling)
methods.summary: Returns a curve that interpolates points on a surface. The interpolant lies on the surface.

params.name: points
params.doc: List of at least two UV parameter locations on the surface.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: Tolerance used for the fit of the push-up curve. Generally, the resulting interpolating curve will be within tolerance of the surface.

params.name: closed
params.doc: If false, the interpolating curve is not closed. If true, the interpolating curve is closed, and the last point and first point should generally not be equal.

params.name: closedSurfaceHandling
params.doc: If 0, all points must be in the rectangular domain of the surface. If the surface is closed in some direction, then this routine will interpret each point and place it at an appropriate location in the covering space. This is the simplest option and should give good results. If 1, then more options for more control of handling curves going across seams are available. If the surface is closed in some direction, then the points are taken as points in the covering space. Example, if srf.IsClosed(0)=true and srf.IsClosed(1)=false and srf.Domain(0)=srf.Domain(1)=Interval(0,1) then if closedSurfaceHandling=1 a point(u, v) in points can have any value for the u coordinate, but must have 0<=v<=1. In particular, if points = { (0.0,0.5), (2.0,0.5) } then the interpolating curve will wrap around the surface two times in the closed direction before ending at start of the curve. If closed=true the last point should equal the first point plus an integer multiple of the period on a closed direction.

returns.doc: A new NURBS curve if successful, or null on error.

methods.name: IsAtSeam
methods.signature: public int IsAtSeam(double u, double v)
methods.summary: Tests if a surface parameter value is at a seam.

params.name: u
params.doc: Surface u parameter to test.

params.name: v
params.doc: Surface v parameter to test.

returns.doc: 0 if not a seam, 1 if u == Domain(0)[i] and srf(u, v) == srf(Domain(0)[1-i], v) 2 if v == Domain(1)[i] and srf(u, v) == srf(u, Domain(1)[1-i]) 3 if 1 and 2 are true.

methods.name: IsAtSingularity
methods.signature: public bool IsAtSingularity(double u, double v, bool exact)
methods.summary: Tests if a surface parameter value is at a singularity.

params.name: u
params.doc: Surface u parameter to test.

params.name: v
params.doc: Surface v parameter to test.

params.name: exact
params.doc: If true, test if (u,v) is exactly at a singularity. If false, test if close enough to cause numerical problems.

returns.doc: true if surface is singular at (s,t)

methods.name: IsClosed
methods.summary: Gets a value indicating if the surface is closed in a direction.

returns.doc: The indicating boolean value.

methods.name: IsCone()
methods.summary: Determines if the surface is a portion of a cone within RhinoMath.ZeroTolerance.

returns.doc: true if the surface is a portion of a cone.

methods.name: IsCone(Double)
methods.summary: Determines if the surface is a portion of a cone within a given tolerance.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: tolerance to use when checking.

returns.doc: true if the surface is a portion of a cone.

methods.name: IsContinuous
methods.signature: public bool IsContinuous(Continuity continuityType, double u, double v)
methods.summary: Tests continuity at a surface parameter value.

params.name: continuityType
params.doc: The continuity type to sample.

params.name: u
params.doc: Surface u parameter to test.

params.name: v
params.doc: Surface v parameter to test.

returns.doc: true if the surface has at least the specified continuity at the (u,v) parameter.

methods.name: IsCylinder()
methods.summary: Determines if the surface is a portion of a cylinder within RhinoMath.ZeroTolerance.

returns.doc: true if the surface is a portion of a cylinder.

methods.name: IsCylinder(Double)
methods.summary: Determines if the surface is a portion of a cylinder within a given tolerance.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: tolerance to use when checking.

returns.doc: true if the surface is a portion of a cylinder.

methods.name: IsIsoparametric(BoundingBox)
methods.summary: Determines if a 2d bounding box is isoparametric in the parameter space of this surface.

params.name: bbox
params.doc: Bounding box to test.

returns.doc: IsoStatus flag describing the iso-parametric relationship between the surface and the bounding box.

methods.name: IsIsoparametric(Curve)
methods.summary: Determines if a 2d curve is isoparametric in the parameter space of this surface.

params.name: curve
params.doc: Curve to test.

returns.doc: IsoStatus flag describing the iso-parametric relationship between the surface and the curve.

methods.name: IsIsoparametric(Curve, Interval)
methods.signature: public IsoStatus IsIsoparametric(Curve curve, Interval curveDomain)
methods.summary: Determines if a 2D curve is isoparametric in the parameter space of this surface.

params.name: curve
params.doc: Curve to test.

params.name: curveDomain
params.doc: Sub domain of the curve.

returns.doc: IsoStatus flag describing the iso-parametric relationship between the surface and the curve.

methods.name: IsoCurve
methods.signature: public Curve IsoCurve(int direction, double constantParameter)
methods.summary: Gets isoparametric curve.
methods.remarks: In this function "direction" indicates which direction the resulting curve runs. 0: horizontal, 1: vertical In the other Surface functions that take a "direction" argument, "direction" indicates if "constantParameter" is a "u" or "v" parameter.

params.name: direction
params.doc: 0 first parameter varies and second parameter is constant e.g., point on IsoCurve(0,c) at t is srf(t,c) This is a horizontal line from left to right 1 first parameter is constant and second parameter varies e.g., point on IsoCurve(1,c) at t is srf(c,t This is a vertical line from bottom to top.

params.name: constantParameter
params.doc: The parameter that was constant on the original surface.

returns.doc: An isoparametric curve or null on error.

methods.name: IsPeriodic
methods.summary: Gets a value indicating if the surface is periodic in a direction (default is false).

returns.doc: The indicating boolean value.

methods.name: IsPlanar()
methods.summary: Tests a surface to see if it is planar to zero tolerance.

returns.doc: true if the surface is planar (flat) to within RhinoMath.ZeroTolerance units (1e-12).

methods.name: IsPlanar(Double)
methods.summary: Tests a surface to see if it is planar to a given tolerance.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: tolerance to use when checking.

returns.doc: true if there is a plane such that the maximum distance from the surface to the plane is <= tolerance.

methods.name: IsSingular
methods.summary: true if surface side is collapsed to a point.

params.name: side
params.doc: side of parameter space to test 0 = south, 1 = east, 2 = north, 3 = west.

returns.doc: True if this specific side of the surface is singular; otherwise, false.

methods.name: IsSphere()
methods.summary: Determines if the surface is a portion of a sphere within RhinoMath.ZeroTolerance.

returns.doc: true if the surface is a portion of a sphere.

methods.name: IsSphere(Double)
methods.summary: Determines if the surface is a portion of a sphere within a given tolerance.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: tolerance to use when checking.

returns.doc: true if the surface is a portion of a sphere.

methods.name: IsTorus()
methods.summary: Determines if the surface is a portion of a torus within RhinoMath.ZeroTolerance.

returns.doc: true if the surface is a portion of a torus.

methods.name: IsTorus(Double)
methods.summary: Determines if the surface is a portion of a torus within a given tolerance.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: tolerance to use when checking.

returns.doc: true if the surface is a portion of a torus.

methods.name: IsValidWithLog
methods.summary: Determines if an object is valid. Also provides a report on errors if this object happens not to be valid.

params.name: log
params.doc: A textual log. This out parameter is assigned during this call.

returns.doc: true if this object is valid; false otherwise.

methods.name: LocalClosestPoint
methods.signature: public bool LocalClosestPoint(Point3d testPoint, double seedU, double seedV, out double u, out double v)
methods.summary: Find parameters of the point on a surface that is locally closest to the testPoint. The search for a local close point starts at seed parameters.

params.name: testPoint
params.doc: A point to test against.

params.name: seedU
params.doc: The seed parameter in the U direction.

params.name: seedV
params.doc: The seed parameter in the V direction.

params.name: u
params.doc: U parameter of the surface that is closest to testPoint.

params.name: v
params.doc: V parameter of the surface that is closest to testPoint.

returns.doc: true if the search is successful, false if the search fails.

methods.name: MakeDeformable
methods.summary: If possible, converts the object into a form that can be accurately modified with "squishy" transformations like projections, shears, an non-uniform scaling.

returns.doc: false if object cannot be converted to a deformable object. true if object was already deformable or was converted into a deformable object.

methods.name: MemoryEstimate
methods.summary: Computes an estimate of the number of bytes that this object is using in memory.

returns.doc: An estimated memory footprint.

methods.name: NonConstOperation
methods.summary: Destroy cache handle

methods.name: NormalAt
methods.signature: public Vector3d NormalAt(double u, double v)
methods.summary: Computes the surface normal at a point. This is the simple evaluation call - it does not support error handling.

params.name: u
params.doc: A U parameter.

params.name: v
params.doc: A V parameter.

returns.doc: The normal.

methods.name: Offset
methods.signature: public Surface Offset(double distance, double tolerance)
methods.summary: Constructs a new surface which is offset from the current surface.

params.name: distance
params.doc: Distance (along surface normal) to offset.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: Offset accuracy.

returns.doc: The offset surface or null on failure.

methods.name: OnSwitchToNonConst
methods.summary: Is called when a non-constant operation occurs.

methods.name: PathLineCurve
methods.summary: Gets the line-like curve that is the conceptual axis of the extrusion.

returns.doc: The path as a line curve.

methods.name: PointAt
methods.signature: public Point3d PointAt(double u, double v)
methods.summary: Evaluates a point at a given parameter.

params.name: u
params.doc: evaluation parameters.

params.name: v
params.doc: evaluation parameters.

returns.doc: Point3d.Unset on failure.

methods.name: Profile3d(ComponentIndex)
methods.summary: Gets one of the profiles.

params.name: ci
params.doc: The index of this profile.

returns.doc: The profile.

methods.name: Profile3d(Int32, Double)
methods.signature: public Curve Profile3d(int profileIndex, double s)
methods.summary: Gets a transversal isocurve of the extruded profile.

params.name: profileIndex
params.doc: 0 <= profileIndex < ProfileCount The outer profile has index 0.

params.name: s
params.doc: 0.0 <= s <= 1.0 A relative parameter controlling which profile is returned. 0 = bottom profile and 1 = top profile.

returns.doc: The profile.

methods.name: ProfileIndex
methods.summary: Gets the index of the profile curve at a domain related to a parameter.

params.name: profileParameter
params.doc: Parameter on profile curve.

returns.doc: -1 if profileParameter does not correspond to a point on the profile curve. When the profileParameter corresponds to the end of one profile and the beginning of the next profile, the index of the next profile is returned.

methods.name: Pullback(Curve, Double)
methods.signature: public Curve Pullback(Curve curve3d, double tolerance)
methods.summary: Pulls a 3d curve back to the surface's parameter space.

params.name: curve3d
params.doc: The curve to pull.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: the maximum acceptable 3d distance between from surface(curve_2d(t)) to the locus of points on the surface that are closest to curve_3d.

returns.doc: 2d curve.

methods.name: Pullback(Curve, Double, Interval)
methods.signature: public Curve Pullback(Curve curve3d, double tolerance, Interval curve3dSubdomain)
methods.summary: Pulls a 3d curve back to the surface's parameter space.

params.name: curve3d
params.doc: A curve.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: the maximum acceptable 3d distance between from surface(curve_2d(t)) to the locus of points on the surface that are closest to curve_3d.

params.name: curve3dSubdomain
params.doc: A sub-domain of the curve to sample.

returns.doc: 2d curve.

methods.name: Pushup(Curve, Double)
methods.signature: public Curve Pushup(Curve curve2d, double tolerance)
methods.summary: Computes a 3d curve that is the composite of a 2d curve and the surface map.

params.name: curve2d
params.doc: a 2d curve whose image is in the surface's domain.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: the maximum acceptable distance from the returned 3d curve to the image of curve_2d on the surface.

returns.doc: 3d curve.

methods.name: Pushup(Curve, Double, Interval)
methods.signature: public Curve Pushup(Curve curve2d, double tolerance, Interval curve2dSubdomain)
methods.summary: Computes a 3d curve that is the composite of a 2d curve and the surface map.

params.name: curve2d
params.doc: a 2d curve whose image is in the surface's domain.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: the maximum acceptable distance from the returned 3d curve to the image of curve_2d on the surface.

params.name: curve2dSubdomain
params.doc: The curve interval (a sub-domain of the original curve) to use.

returns.doc: 3d curve.

methods.name: Rebuild
methods.signature: public NurbsSurface Rebuild(int uDegree, int vDegree, int uPointCount, int vPointCount)
methods.summary: Rebuilds an existing surface to a given degree and point count.

params.name: uDegree
params.doc: the output surface u degree.

params.name: vDegree
params.doc: the output surface u degree.

params.name: uPointCount
params.doc: The number of points in the output surface u direction. Must be bigger than uDegree (maximum value is 1000)

params.name: vPointCount
params.doc: The number of points in the output surface v direction. Must be bigger than vDegree (maximum value is 1000)

returns.doc: new rebuilt surface on success. null on failure.

methods.name: RebuildOneDirection
methods.signature: public NurbsSurface RebuildOneDirection(int direction, int pointCount, LoftType loftType, double refitTolerance)
methods.summary: Rebuilds an existing surface with a new surface to a given point count in either the u or v directions independently.

params.name: direction
params.doc: The direction (0 = U, 1 = V).

params.name: pointCount
params.doc: The number of points in the output surface in the "direction" direction.

params.name: loftType
params.doc: The loft type

params.name: refitTolerance
params.doc: The refit tolerance. When in doubt, use the document's model absolute tolerance.

returns.doc: new rebuilt surface on success. null on failure.

methods.name: Reverse(Int32)
methods.summary: Reverses parameterization Domain changes from [a,b] to [-b,-a]

params.name: direction
params.doc: 0 for first parameter's domain, 1 for second parameter's domain.

returns.doc: a new reversed surface on success.

methods.name: Reverse(Int32, Boolean)
methods.signature: public Surface Reverse(int direction, bool inPlace)
methods.summary: Same as Reverse, but if inPlace is set to true this Surface is modified instead of a new copy being created.

params.name: direction
params.doc: 0 for first parameter's domain, 1 for second parameter's domain.

params.name: inPlace
params.doc: [Missing <param name="inPlace"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Surface.Reverse(System.Int32,System.Boolean)"]

returns.doc: If inPlace is False, a new reversed surface on success. If inPlace is true, this surface instance is returned on success.

methods.name: Rotate
methods.signature: public bool Rotate(double angleRadians, Vector3d rotationAxis, Point3d rotationCenter)
methods.summary: Rotates the object about the specified axis. A positive rotation angle results in a counter-clockwise rotation about the axis (right hand rule).

params.name: angleRadians
params.doc: Angle of rotation in radians.

params.name: rotationAxis
params.doc: Direction of the axis of rotation.

params.name: rotationCenter
params.doc: Point on the axis of rotation.

returns.doc: true if geometry successfully rotated.

methods.name: Scale
methods.summary: Scales the object by the specified factor. The scale is centered at the origin.

params.name: scaleFactor
params.doc: The uniform scaling factor.

returns.doc: true if geometry successfully scaled.

methods.name: SetDomain
methods.signature: public virtual bool SetDomain(int direction, Interval domain)
methods.summary: Sets the domain in a direction.

params.name: direction
params.doc: 0 sets first parameter's domain, 1 sets second parameter's domain.

params.name: domain
params.doc: A new domain to be assigned.

returns.doc: true if setting succeeded, otherwise false.

methods.name: SetOuterProfile
methods.signature: public bool SetOuterProfile(Curve outerProfile, bool cap)
methods.summary: Sets the outer profile of the extrusion.

params.name: outerProfile
params.doc: curve in the XY plane or a 2D curve.

params.name: cap
params.doc: If outerProfile is a closed curve, then cap determines if the extrusion has end caps. If outerProfile is an open curve, cap is ignored.

returns.doc: true if the profile was set. If the outer profile is closed, then the extrusion may also have inner profiles. If the outer profile is open, the extrusion may not have inner profiles. If the extrusion already has a profile, the set will fail.

methods.name: SetPathAndUp
methods.signature: public bool SetPathAndUp(Point3d a, Point3d b, Vector3d up)
methods.summary: Allows to set the two points at the extremes and the up vector.

params.name: a
params.doc: The start point.

params.name: b
params.doc: The end point.

params.name: up
params.doc: The up vector.

returns.doc: true if the operation succeeded; otherwise false. Setting up=a-b will make the operation fail.

methods.name: SetUserString
methods.signature: public bool SetUserString(string key, string value)
methods.summary: Attach a user string (key,value combination) to this geometry.

params.name: key
params.doc: id used to retrieve this string.

params.name: value
params.doc: string associated with key.

returns.doc: true on success.

methods.name: ShortPath
methods.signature: public Curve ShortPath(Point2d start, Point2d end, double tolerance)
methods.summary: Constructs a geodesic between 2 points, used by ShortPath command in Rhino.

params.name: start
params.doc: start point of curve in parameter space. Points must be distinct in the domain of the surface.

params.name: end
params.doc: end point of curve in parameter space. Points must be distinct in the domain of the surface.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: tolerance used in fitting discrete solution.

returns.doc: a geodesic curve on the surface on success. null on failure.

methods.name: Smooth(Double, Boolean, Boolean, Boolean, Boolean, SmoothingCoordinateSystem)
methods.signature: public Surface Smooth(double smoothFactor, bool bXSmooth, bool bYSmooth, bool bZSmooth, bool bFixBoundaries, SmoothingCoordinateSystem coordinateSystem)
methods.summary: Smooths a surface by averaging the positions of control points in a specified region.

params.name: smoothFactor
params.doc: The smoothing factor, which controls how much control points move towards the average of the neighboring control points.

params.name: bXSmooth
params.doc: When true control points move in X axis direction.

params.name: bYSmooth
params.doc: When true control points move in Y axis direction.

params.name: bZSmooth
params.doc: When true control points move in Z axis direction.

params.name: bFixBoundaries
params.doc: When true the surface edges don't move.

params.name: coordinateSystem
params.doc: The coordinates to determine the direction of the smoothing.

returns.doc: The smoothed surface if successful, null otherwise.

methods.name: Smooth(Double, Boolean, Boolean, Boolean, Boolean, SmoothingCoordinateSystem, Plane)
methods.signature: public Surface Smooth(double smoothFactor, bool bXSmooth, bool bYSmooth, bool bZSmooth, bool bFixBoundaries, SmoothingCoordinateSystem coordinateSystem, Plane plane)
methods.summary: Smooths a surface by averaging the positions of control points in a specified region.

params.name: smoothFactor
params.doc: The smoothing factor, which controls how much control points move towards the average of the neighboring control points.

params.name: bXSmooth
params.doc: When true control points move in X axis direction.

params.name: bYSmooth
params.doc: When true control points move in Y axis direction.

params.name: bZSmooth
params.doc: When true control points move in Z axis direction.

params.name: bFixBoundaries
params.doc: When true the surface edges don't move.

params.name: coordinateSystem
params.doc: The coordinates to determine the direction of the smoothing.

params.name: plane
params.doc: If SmoothingCoordinateSystem.CPlane specified, then the construction plane.

returns.doc: The smoothed surface if successful, null otherwise.

methods.name: SpanCount
methods.summary: Gets number of smooth nonempty spans in the parameter direction.

params.name: direction
params.doc: 0 gets first parameter's domain, 1 gets second parameter's domain.

returns.doc: The span count.

methods.name: Split
methods.signature: public Surface[] Split(int direction, double parameter)
methods.summary: Splits (divides) the surface into two parts at the specified parameter

params.name: direction
params.doc: 0 = The surface is split vertically. The "west" side is returned as the first surface in the array and the "east" side is returned as the second surface in the array. 1 = The surface is split horizontally. The "south" side is returned as the first surface in the array and the "north" side is returned as the second surface in the array

params.name: parameter
params.doc: value of constant parameter in interval returned by Domain(direction)

returns.doc: Array of two surfaces on success

methods.name: ToBrep()
methods.summary: Converts the surface into a Brep.

returns.doc: A Brep with a similar shape like this surface or null.

methods.name: ToBrep(Boolean)
methods.summary: Constructs a brep form of the extrusion. The outer profile is always the first face of the brep. If there are inner profiles, additional brep faces are created for each profile. If the outer profile is closed, then end caps are added as the last two faces of the brep.

params.name: splitKinkyFaces
params.doc: If true and the profiles have kinks, then the faces corresponding to those profiles are split so they will be G1.

returns.doc: A brep with a similar shape like this extrusion, or null on error.

methods.name: ToJSON
methods.summary: Create a JSON string representation of this object

params.name: options
params.doc: [Missing <param name="options"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Runtime.CommonObject.ToJSON(Rhino.FileIO.SerializationOptions)"]

returns.doc: [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Runtime.CommonObject.ToJSON(Rhino.FileIO.SerializationOptions)"]

methods.name: ToNurbsSurface()
methods.summary: Gets a NURBS surface representation of this surface. Default tolerance of 0.0 is used.

returns.doc: NurbsSurface on success, null on failure.

methods.name: ToNurbsSurface(Double, Int32)
methods.signature: public NurbsSurface ToNurbsSurface(double tolerance, out int accuracy)
methods.summary: Gets a NURBS surface representation of this surface.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: tolerance to use when creating NURBS representation.

params.name: accuracy
params.doc: 0 = unable to create NURBS representation with desired accuracy. 1 = success - returned NURBS parameterization matches the surface's to the desired accuracy. 2 = success - returned NURBS point locus matches the surface's to the desired accuracy and the domain of the NURBS surface is correct. However, this surface's parameterization and the NURBS surface parameterization may not match to the desired accuracy. This situation happens when getting NURBS representations of surfaces that have a transcendental parameterization like spheres, cylinders, and cones.

returns.doc: NurbsSurface on success, null on failure.

methods.name: Transform
methods.summary: Transforms the geometry. If the input Transform has a SimilarityType of OrientationReversing, you may want to consider flipping the transformed geometry after calling this function when it makes sense. For example, you may want to call Flip() on a Brep after transforming it.

params.name: xform
params.doc: Transformation to apply to geometry.

returns.doc: true if geometry successfully transformed.

methods.name: Translate(Vector3d)
methods.summary: Translates the object along the specified vector.

params.name: translationVector
params.doc: A moving vector.

returns.doc: true if geometry successfully translated.

methods.name: Translate(Double, Double, Double)
methods.signature: public bool Translate(double x, double y, double z)
methods.summary: Translates the object along the specified vector.

params.name: x
params.doc: The X component.

params.name: y
params.doc: The Y component.

params.name: z
params.doc: The Z component.

returns.doc: true if geometry successfully translated.

methods.name: Transpose()
methods.summary: Transposes surface parameterization (swap U and V)

returns.doc: New transposed surface on success, null on failure.

methods.name: Transpose(Boolean)
methods.summary: Transposes surface parameterization (swap U and V)

params.name: inPlace
params.doc: [Missing <param name="inPlace"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Surface.Transpose(System.Boolean)"]

returns.doc: New transposed surface on success, null on failure.

methods.name: Trim
methods.signature: public Surface Trim(Interval u, Interval v)
methods.summary: Constructs a sub-surface that covers the specified UV trimming domain.

params.name: u
params.doc: Domain of surface along U direction to include in the subsurface.

params.name: v
params.doc: Domain of surface along V direction to include in the subsurface.

returns.doc: SubSurface on success, null on failure.

methods.name: TryGetCone(Cone)
methods.summary: Tests a surface to see if it is a portion of a cone within RhinoMath.ZeroTolerance and return the cone.

params.name: cone
params.doc: On success, the cone parameters are filled in.

returns.doc: true if the surface is a portion of a cone.

methods.name: TryGetCone(Cone, Double)
methods.signature: public bool TryGetCone(out Cone cone, double tolerance)
methods.summary: Tests a surface to see if it is a portion of a cone and returns the cone.

params.name: cone
params.doc: On success, the cone parameters are filled in.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: tolerance to use when checking.

returns.doc: true if the surface is a portion of a cone.

methods.name: TryGetCylinder(Cylinder)
methods.summary: Tests a surface to see if it is a portion of a cylinder within RhinoMath.ZeroTolerance and return the cylinder.
methods.remarks: If successful, an infinite cylinder is returned. When a surface has a cylindrical shape, even if its ends are not circles on the cylinder, it returns the axis and radius. You can detect infinite cylinders using Cylinder.IsFinite.

params.name: cylinder
params.doc: On success, the cylinder parameters are filled in.

returns.doc: true if the surface is a portion of a cylinder.

methods.name: TryGetCylinder(Cylinder, Double)
methods.signature: public bool TryGetCylinder(out Cylinder cylinder, double tolerance)
methods.summary: Tests a surface to see if it is a portion of a cylinder and return the infinite cylinder.
methods.remarks: If successful, an infinite cylinder is returned. When a surface has a cylindrical shape, even if its ends are not circles on the cylinder, it returns the axis and radius. You can detect infinite cylinders using Cylinder.IsFinite.

params.name: cylinder
params.doc: On success, the cylinder parameters are filled in.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: tolerance to use when checking.

returns.doc: true if the surface is a portion of a cylinder.

methods.name: TryGetFiniteCylinder
methods.signature: public bool TryGetFiniteCylinder(out Cylinder cylinder, double tolerance)
methods.summary: Tests a surface with the assumption that it might be a right circular cylinder and returns this geometry.
methods.remarks: If successful, a finite cylinder is returned.

params.name: cylinder
params.doc: On success, the cylinder parameters are filled in.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: tolerance to use when checking.

returns.doc: true if the surface is a portion of a cylinder.

methods.name: TryGetPlane(Plane)
methods.summary: Tests a surface for planarity and return the plane.

params.name: plane
params.doc: On success, the plane parameters are filled in.

returns.doc: true if there is a plane such that the maximum distance from the surface to the plane is <= RhinoMath.ZeroTolerance.

methods.name: TryGetPlane(Plane, Double)
methods.signature: public bool TryGetPlane(out Plane plane, double tolerance)
methods.summary: Tests a surface for planarity and return the plane.

params.name: plane
params.doc: On success, the plane parameters are filled in.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: tolerance to use when checking.

returns.doc: true if there is a plane such that the maximum distance from the surface to the plane is <= tolerance.

methods.name: TryGetSphere(Sphere)
methods.summary: Test a surface to see if it is a portion of a sphere and return the sphere.

params.name: sphere
params.doc: On success, the sphere parameters are filled in.

returns.doc: true if the surface is a portion of a sphere.

methods.name: TryGetSphere(Sphere, Double)
methods.signature: public bool TryGetSphere(out Sphere sphere, double tolerance)
methods.summary: Test a surface to see if it is a portion of a sphere and return the sphere.

params.name: sphere
params.doc: On success, the sphere parameters are filled in.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: tolerance to use when checking.

returns.doc: true if the surface is a portion of a sphere.

methods.name: TryGetTorus(Torus)
methods.summary: Tests a surface to see if it is a portion of a torus within RhinoMath.ZeroTolerance and returns the torus.

params.name: torus
params.doc: On success, the torus parameters are filled in.

returns.doc: true if the surface is a portion of a torus.

methods.name: TryGetTorus(Torus, Double)
methods.signature: public bool TryGetTorus(out Torus torus, double tolerance)
methods.summary: Tests a surface to see if it is a portion of a torus and returns the torus.

params.name: torus
params.doc: On success, the torus parameters are filled in.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: tolerance to use when checking.

returns.doc: true if the surface is a portion of a torus.

methods.name: VariableOffset(Double, Double, Double, Double, Double)
methods.signature: public Surface VariableOffset(double uMinvMin, double uMinvMax, double uMaxvMin, double uMaxvMax, double tolerance)
methods.summary: Copies a surface so that all locations at the corners of the copied surface are specified distances from the original surface.

params.name: uMinvMin
params.doc: Offset distance at Domain(0).Min, Domain(1).Min.

params.name: uMinvMax
params.doc: Offset distance at Domain(0).Min, Domain(1).Max.

params.name: uMaxvMin
params.doc: Offset distance at Domain(0).Max, Domain(1).Min.

params.name: uMaxvMax
params.doc: Offset distance at Domain(0).Max, Domain(1).Max.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: The offset tolerance.

returns.doc: The offset surface if successful, null otherwise.

methods.name: VariableOffset(Double, Double, Double, Double, IEnumerable<Point2d>, IEnumerable<Double>, Double)
methods.signature: public Surface VariableOffset(double uMinvMin, double uMinvMax, double uMaxvMin, double uMaxvMax, IEnumerable<Point2d> interiorParameters, IEnumerable<double> interiorDistances, double tolerance)
methods.summary: Copies a surface so that all locations at the corners, and from specified interior locations, of the copied surface are specified distances from the original surface.

params.name: uMinvMin
params.doc: Offset distance at Domain(0).Min, Domain(1).Min.

params.name: uMinvMax
params.doc: Offset distance at Domain(0).Min, Domain(1).Max.

params.name: uMaxvMin
params.doc: Offset distance at Domain(0).Max, Domain(1).Min.

params.name: uMaxvMax
params.doc: Offset distance at Domain(0).Max, Domain(1).Max.

params.name: interiorParameters
params.doc: An array of interior UV parameters to offset from.

params.name: interiorDistances
params.doc: >An array of offset distances at the interior UV parameters.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: The offset tolerance.

returns.doc: The offset surface if successful, null otherwise.

methods.name: WallEdge
methods.summary: Gets one of the longitudinal curves along the beam or extrusion.

params.name: ci
params.doc: The index of this profile.

returns.doc: The profile.

methods.name: WallSurface
methods.summary: Gets one of the longitudinal surfaces of the extrusion.

params.name: ci
params.doc: The index specifying which precise item to retrieve.

returns.doc: The surface.

properties.name: CapCount
properties.summary: Gets the amount of capping surfaces.

properties.name: Disposed
properties.summary: Indicates if this object has been disposed or the document it originally belonged to has been disposed.

properties.name: HasBrepForm
properties.summary: Returns true if the Brep.TryConvertBrep function will be successful for this object

properties.name: HasUserData
properties.summary: Gets true if this class has any custom information attached to it through UserData.

properties.name: IsCappedAtBottom
properties.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the surface that fills the bottom profile is existing.

properties.name: IsCappedAtTop
properties.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the surface that fills the top profile is existing.

properties.name: IsDeformable
properties.summary: true if object can be accurately modified with "squishy" transformations like projections, shears, and non-uniform scaling.

properties.name: IsDocumentControlled
properties.summary: If true this object may not be modified. Any properties or functions that attempt to modify this object when it is set to "IsReadOnly" will throw a NotSupportedException.

properties.name: IsMiteredAtEnd
properties.summary: Gets a value indicating whether a miter plane at the end is defined.

properties.name: IsMiteredAtStart
properties.summary: Returns a value indicating whether a miter plane at start is defined.

properties.name: IsSolid
properties.summary: Gets a value indicating whether there is no gap among all surfaces constructing this object.

properties.name: IsSubDFriendly
properties.summary: Returns true if the surface is a non-rational, uniform, natural or periodic, cubic NURBS surface. Otherwise, false is returend.

properties.name: IsValid
properties.summary: Tests an object to see if it is valid.

properties.name: MiterPlaneNormalAtEnd
properties.summary: Gets or sets the normal of the miter plane at the end in profile coordinates. In profile coordinates, 0,0,1 always maps to the extrusion axis

properties.name: MiterPlaneNormalAtStart
properties.summary: Gets or sets the normal of the miter plane at the start in profile coordinates. In profile coordinates, 0,0,1 always maps to the extrusion axis

properties.name: ObjectType
properties.summary: Useful for switch statements that need to differentiate between basic object types like points, curves, surfaces, and so on.

properties.name: PathEnd
properties.summary: Gets the end point of the path.

properties.name: PathStart
properties.summary: Gets the start point of the path.

properties.name: PathTangent
properties.summary: Gets the up vector of the path.

properties.name: ProfileCount
properties.summary: Gets the amount of profile curves.

properties.name: UserData
properties.summary: List of custom information that is attached to this class.

properties.name: UserDictionary
properties.summary: Dictionary of custom information attached to this class. The dictionary is actually user data provided as an easy to use sharable set of information.

properties.name: UserStringCount
properties.summary: Gets the amount of user strings.
